chunks.page_summary: This page introduces the USB connection capabilities for Rheonics SME (sensor electronics) and notes the use of a standard USB micro B port.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Introducing USB connections on the Rheonics SME
(sensor electronics)

Modified on: Thu, 3 Jul, 2025 at 5:49 PM

All Rheonics sensor electronics (SME) come with standard USB micro B port.
Connecting USB cable to SME-DRM
SME-DRM
key_facts: All Rheonics sensor electronics (SME) are equipped with a standard USB micro B port.
The document mentions connecting a USB cable to SME-DRM.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays a detailed view of a Rheonics SME-DRM module, highlighting its various ports, connectors, indicators, and identification markings.
key_facts: The device is a Rheonics SME-DRM module.
It features multiple communication interfaces including Modbus RS485, Ethernet TCP/IP, and Modbus TCP.
The module provides a 5V DC power input and a 4-20 mA analog output with three channels.
It includes status indicators for power, sensor, communication, and data.
Connectivity options consist of a USB port, an SD card slot, a display connector, and an 8-pin sensor connection.
The product is identified by part number PN:1917-BO and serial number SN:BFCM-R02-19101590.
The module is CE compliant and manufactured in Switzerland.
A Micro-USB cable is used with the SME-DRM.
warnings_notes: CE marking indicates compliance with European health, safety, and environmental protection standards.

key_facts: The device is a Rheonics SME-DRM module.
It features status LEDs for power, sensor, communication, and data.
It supports Modbus RS485 communication.
The module includes a USB port, SD-card slot, and a display connector.
It provides 4-20 mA analog outputs across three channels (ch1, ch2, ch3).
The device is identified by part number PN:1917-BO/SN:BFCM-R02-19.
The product is labeled as 'swiss made'.

Connecting USB cable to SME-TRD and SME-TR
refer to article on connecting USB cable to transmitter
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297828)
key_facts: The image displays the various connectivity options and status indicators of a Rheonics transmitter device.
The device features multiple communication interfaces, including Modbus (RS-232), Ethernet (TCP/IP, Modbus TCP), and USB.
It provides a 4-20 mA analog output with connections for loop+, ground, and three channels (ch1+, ch2+, ch3+).
A dedicated 7-pin connector is present for sensor connection.
LED indicators signal the status of power (green), sensor (red), communication, and data activity.
The device is identified as 'swiss made' and bears the 'CE' compliance mark, with a partially visible part number P/N: 1812-0050.
Instructions for connecting a USB cable to SME-TRD and SME-TR transmitters are referenced via a link to a Rheonics support article.
warnings_notes: Refer to article on connecting USB cable to transmitter for detailed instructions.
